Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

How many of your answers got deleted...or your account?

Other · asked by user [] · 2007-10-15 · 8 answers
In other words,what seem to disburb most the moderators?
user [188] · 2007-10-15
None. I guess Im not disruptive enough.
user [1] · 2007-10-15
"Pariprashnena.com support"
to Gaurangalila,

Oct 14 (1 day ago)

Dear Gaurangalila das:

We have deleted your post: What is Srila Prabhupadas instructions
regarding selling of Iskcons farms?

Although we might be or not sympathetic with some rightful cause like
Costa Rica farm please understand that pariprashnena.com is not the
place to start a controversy about it.

It is for questions and answers, not for advancing ANY cause, be anti,
pro, rightful, hateful or whatever.

Thank you for your understanding.

Your servants,
user [154] · 2007-10-18
janannath sutta prabhu has deleted a few, I think he was most disturbed by the suggestion that Srila Prabhupada had spent time with a woman (to produce his children of course) - would you reject him just because of this? The suggestion was taken as an insult, strange, but Im greatful to admin anyway.

I like when my posts are deleted, it makes me feel that Im not in control.

ys

ccd
user [1] · 2007-10-18
"Pariprashnena.com support"
to ccd,

sent Sep 13, 2007

Dear Ccd,

We have noticed you are sometimes posting out of context.
and you posted this:

***Would you reject Prabhupada if he spent a night with a woman? ***

We allow any question, we do not censor any theme, but we DO have zero tolerance with distasteful and out of context commentaries for obvious reasons.

Please follow the simple rules of the forum.

Thank you for your contributions,

your servants,
user [40] · 2007-10-18
Dear friends

This is not aimed at any particular person, it is for me as much as anyone

Please accept my humble obeisances, All glories to Srila Prabhupada!!

I have only had one post deleted, and it wasnt out of any other desire on Sri Admin but to keep things tidy and in context. I was asked to start a new question with it instead. I chose not to (lazy) I type a lot then delete it before I submit, its a good practice. Its all in the editing, not in the writing.

I have spent years on internet based forums since 1994. If you dont understand what a forum is for, and how it works and the enormous task of the Admin devas to maintain order and context then you will run foul of the process. I have seen it thousands of times. i have seen forums go wild and crash because of the hidden bias of many of the co-respondents

I think part of the problem with some is the indirect method. If you see or have an issue then say it. If you ask an indirect question its usually a veiled attempt to get support for your own thinking. Its not a fair way to ask a question. If you dont want to directly ask your question then you should seriously consider whether it should be asked at all.

If you are wanting to ask a question to "provoke" a response this is not the place (that is my reading of what this forum is for) If you have a statement to make this is not the place. If you have an agenda to run this is not the place.

This is the place to ask questions in a humble manner that will assist you develop your Krishna Consciousness.

If someone is breaking the law call the cops dont complain about it here. If its not against the law and you dont like it then try and influence the people doing it. There are people here who will happily describe how to go about that.

This is not the X-files a place for conspiracy theory its a place to INQUIRE submissively: Dear friends, please help me with this particular issue is they general rule to apply.

Lastly this is an archive of questions and answer, A RESOURCE FOR ALL DEVOTEES it says. It is a place for uplift and clarification of things, so that we can learn and later someone else with the same question will find the answer. You should keep that in mind when you are posting. 500 million internet users could look here and see it all. And YOU connected to it. Consider that before you post our next question.

I like it here as I am a child in these things and I learn a lot from my worthy teachers. Good on you teachers, I am grateful.

Sorry to rant.

abrennan
user [19] · 2007-10-19
Abrennan Prabhu ki!!!!
JAYA!!!!
user [40] · 2007-10-19
Good on you mates
